review_questions: Does CP00-145 cover exactly the 40 planned Risk B permission matrix workflow boundary units in order?

Can any CP00-145 row evaluate permission matrix, view, search, mutation, export/download, share, AI retrieval, legal hold, ethical wall, object ACL, review, approval, trimming, or audit event behavior?
Can any CP00-145 row execute allowed, denied, cross-tenant, or leak-prevention tests rather than describing them?
Are hidden permission rules, route reasons, object ACL grants, unauthorized counts, and unauthorized object names suppressed?
Does CP00-145 hand off cleanly from CP00-144 to CP00-146 without implementing LDIP or splitting HRX?

parsed_review.adjudication_notes:
Read-only review of the CP00-145 staged diff against all five focus areas. No P0-P3 defects found; PASS.

FOCUS 1 (exactly 40 Risk B units, in order) — PASS. buildRows generates from three micro-phases: M03 start=20/count=3 -> slice(19,22) yields index 20,21,22 (S20-S22); M04 default start=1/count=22 -> S01-S22; M05 start=1/count=15 -> S01-S15. Total 3+22+15=40, first=RP03.P06.M03.S20, last=RP03.P06.M05.S15, matching AUDIT_COMPLIANCE_CP145_PACK_BINDING and the contract covered_range. validateAuditComplianceCp145Coverage compares each covered ID positionally against plan included_units; tests assert coverage.ok with empty errors and the validator reports covered_units 40 (exit 0), confirming order and count match the plan. IDs are unique (Set size check).

FOCUS 2 (no permission/decision/workflow evaluation) — PASS. Rows are pure frozen metadata; runCase only locates a row and returns descriptors. freezeNoWriteResult forces evaluates_permission_matrix/view/search/mutation/export_download/share/ai_retrieval_decision=false, applies_legal_hold/ethical_wall=false, reads_object_acl=false, routes_review_required/approval_required=false, proves_security_trimming=false, emits_audit_event_expectation=false, writes_permission_fixture=false. Contract no_write_attestation mirrors these and the validator enforces all === false.

FOCUS 3 (tests described, not executed) — PASS. denied/cross-tenant/leak/allowed rows carry domain permission_matrix_test_reference and deliverable_type test, but executes_allowed_test/denied_test/cross_tenant_test/leak_prevention_test=false on every result; validator asserts the same for the nine sampled cases.

FOCUS 4 (hidden rules/reasons/grants/counts/object names suppressed) — PASS. Internal predicates, policies, route reasons, object_acl_internal_grant, security_trimming_internal_query, audit_event_internal_payload, cross_tenant_row_count, and unauthorized_object_name appear only as denylist field-name tokens in CP145_HIDDEN_SOURCE_FIELDS; no actual values are materialized. Case results set unauthorized_count_exposed/unauthorized_object_name_exposed/hidden_field_names_exposed=false and contract exposes_unauthorized_count/object_name=false; validator checks the forbidden-name list and these flags.

FOCUS 5 (clean CP00-144 -> CP00-146 handoff, no LDIP, no HRX split) — PASS. Binding upstream=CP00-144, next=CP00-146, next_subphase=RP03.P06.M05.S16; CP00-145 first unit S20 resumes exactly where CP00-144 declared (RP03.P06.M03.S20). Handoff packet and contract current_pack agree; implements_ldip=false and splits_hrx_product=false throughout. Schema bumped v0.11->v0.12 with the validator's expected-version check updated to match.

Deliverable distribution (test 7 / security_audit 7 / implementation 16 / ui 8 / claude_review 2 = 40) and evidence-mode distribution (3/22/15) were re-derived by hand and match the catalog and validator assertions.

Observation (non-blocking): the CP00-145 Hermes packet evidence_refs lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-145/manifest.json, which is not part of this diff. This is by-design and consistent with prior packs — both the test and validator resolve cp145Plan via the plan with a `?? manifest.plan_binding_snapshot` fallback, so manifest absence is tolerated and the validator passes from the plan alone. Not a defect.
